records.question: Which departments are located in Linderman with a budget of at most 601283.6, where instructor Desyl earns exactly 48803.38 and student Porter has at most 111 total credits?
records.gold_sql:
SELECT department.dept_name FROM department
JOIN instructor ON department.dept_name = instructor.dept_name
JOIN student ON department.dept_name = student.dept_name
WHERE student.name = 'Porter' AND instructor.salary = 48803.38 AND department.building = 'Linderman' AND department.budget <= 601283.6 AND instructor.name = 'Desyl' AND student.tot_cred <= 111
records.pred_sql: select department.dept_name from department join instructor on department.dept_name = instructor.dept_name join student on department.dept_name = student.dept_name where lower(department.building) = 'linderman' and department.budget <= 601283.6 and lower(instructor.name) = 'desyl' and instructor.salary = 48803.38 and lower(student.name) = 'porter' and student.tot_cred <= 111 limit 5;
records.thought: We need departments in 'Linderman' with budget <= 601283.6, having an instructor named 'Desyl' with salary 48803.38, and a student named 'Porter' with tot_cred <= 111. We'll join department with instructor and student on dept_name, and apply all filters.

records.question: Which departments are located in the Saucon building and have an instructor named Lembr teaching the 4-credit course titled Quantum Mechanics, with students having at most 113 total credits?
records.gold_sql:
SELECT department.dept_name FROM department
JOIN course ON department.dept_name = course.dept_name
JOIN instructor ON department.dept_name = instructor.dept_name
JOIN student ON department.dept_name = student.dept_name
WHERE instructor.name = 'Lembr' AND course.credits = 4 AND course.title = 'Quantum Mechanics' AND department.building = 'Saucon' AND student.tot_cred <= 113
records.pred_sql: select distinct department.dept_name from department join instructor on department.dept_name = instructor.dept_name join teaches on instructor.ID = teaches.ID join section on teaches.course_id = section.course_id and teaches.sec_id = section.sec_id and teaches.semester = section.semester and teaches.year = section.year join course on section.course_id = course.course_id join takes on section.course_id = takes.course_id and section.sec_id = takes.sec_id and section.semester = takes.semester and section.year = takes.year join student on takes.ID = student.ID where lower(department.building) = 'saucon' and lower(instructor.name) like '%lembr%' and lower(course.title) like '%quantum mechanics%' and course.credits = 4 and student.tot_cred <= 113 limit 5;
records.thought: We need departments in the 'Saucon' building, with an instructor named 'Lembr' teaching a 4-credit course titled 'Quantum Mechanics', and students in that department having at most 113 total credits. This requires joining department, instructor, course, teaches, section, takes, and student tables.
